top_signals.title: The Electric Grid Is 'Not Going To Work' As Is For Data Centers Says FERC's Swett
top_signals.summary: The chair of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission Laura Swett joined Balance of Power joined to discuss the power demand for datacenters. She said interventionist and unprecedented action will be needed to connect power-hungry data centers to US grids while protecting consumers,Without changes, the country’s electric grids are “not going to work.”  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Christine Lagarde, Boris Vujčić: Monetary policy statement (with Q&A)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp
top_signals.summary: International law enforcement agencies cleaned nearly 15,000 malware-infected WordPress websites and took down more than 100 servers linked to the SocGholish botnet and the Evil Corp Russian cybercrime group. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Kenya to Raise $763 Million After Lawmakers Tone Down Tax Plan
top_signals.summary: Kenyan lawmakers watered down a slew of tax proposals fronted by the Treasury that would have raised nearly $1 billion for the East African nation’s budget.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Fair winds, INDOPACOM: Pentagon returns command name to US Pacific Command
top_signals.summary: U.S. Pacific Command was the formation's official name until 2018, when it was changed to U.S. Indo-Pacific Command.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: How FIFA Picks World Cup Host Cities | Sports City
top_signals.summary: FIFA is projected to pull in a staggering $10.9 billion from the World Cup—a 56% jump from the last tournament. Meanwhile, the host cities and stadiums don't see a single dime from ticket sales or sponsorships. Bloomberg’s Randall Williams takes you inside the intense bids, the multimillion-dollar stadium upgrades and why cities still fight for the chaos.  (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Solar generation in CAISO surpassed natural gas in the first five months of 2026
top_signals.summary: In the first five months of 2026, utility-scale solar generation surpassed natural gas generation in CAISO. Solar electricity generation in the California Independent System Operator (CAISO) over the first five months of 2026 increased 21% compared with the same period in 2024, and natural gas generation decreased by 60%, data from our Hourly Electric Grid Monitor shows.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
